dev.jeka.core.api.depmanagement.artifact

Class JkArtifactId

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.depmanagement.artifact.JkArtifactId

  • public final class JkArtifactId
extends java.lang.Object
    Artifacts are files produced by projects in order to be published and reused by other projects. JkArtifactId identifies artifacts within a project.

    The identifier is compound of a classifier and a file extension. The classifier maps with the Maven 'classifier' concept.
    In a project, we distinguish the main artifact from the others : the main artifact classifier values to empty string.

    • Method Detail

      • of

        public static JkArtifactId of(java.lang.String classifier,
                              java.lang.String extension)
        Creates an artifact id with the specified classifier and extension.

        The classifier cannot be null or be a string composed of spaces. An empty string extension generally means that the file has no extension.

      • ofMainArtifact

        public static JkArtifactId ofMainArtifact(java.lang.String extension)
        Shorthand for of(MAIN_ARTIFACT_NAME, String).

      • isMainArtifact

        public boolean isMainArtifact()

      • getClassifier

        public java.lang.String getClassifier()
        Returns the classifier of this object.

      • getExtension

        public java.lang.String getExtension()
        Returns the file getExtension of this object.

      • toFileName

        public java.lang.String toFileName(java.lang.String namePart)
        Returns the filename of this artifactId, specifying the base filename for computing the final name.
        Parameters:
        namePart - The base name for computing the complete filename of the artifact. This is generally the name (or moduleId) of the project generating this artifact.

      • equals

        public boolean equals(java.lang.Object o)
        Overrides:

      • toSquareNotation

        public java.lang.String toSquareNotation()
        Returns a representation of the artifact id in the format "[classifier, extension]".
